setting.note: Oceania's capital is the London Orwell knew in 1948: bomb-scarred, rationed and grey, its landmarks renamed and repurposed by the Party. He built the ministries and squares of Airstrip One from real buildings he had worked in and walked past, so the fictional city maps onto the actual one.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. George Orwell, 1949. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Nineteen Eighty-Four, George Orwell (1949). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The cold slate-grey of concrete and telescreens, the colour of Orwell's rationed, overcast London. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper at about 10:1.
